response:
# Search Results Page with Live-Updating Count Accessibility Design Plan > **For Claude:** Use a11y-planner protocol. Review with a11y-critic after implementation. > **Compliance target:** WCAG 2.2 AA > **Users who need accessibility:** Screen reader users, keyboard-only users, low vision users, users with cognitive load sensitivity > **Assistive technologies:** NVDA, JAWS, VoiceOver, keyboard-only navigation **Feature:** A legal research search results page where search input, autocomplete, filters, sort, loading state, result count, and paginated result cards update dynamically. **Risk Level:** Medium **Component/Page Type:** Search page with autocomplete, live status updates, filter chips, native select sort, async results list --- ## Scope & Context This plan covers the accessible implementation of the search results page, with emphasis on live result count announcements, async loading state communication, filter chip state, sort control semantics, and result list structure. The main accessibility risk is not complex ARIA widgets, but dynamic state changes that can become silent, noisy, or disruptive for assistive technology users. The result count updates after a 300ms debounce, filters and sort trigger immediate re-queries, and multiple state changes can happen together. The design must announce meaningful status changes without moving focus or flooding screen reader queues. This plan does not claim to cover the full legal research domain taxonomy, search ranking explanation, account/authentication flows, or PDF/document-reader accessibility. It covers the search results page interaction model and dynamic result state. --- ## Semantic Structure Plan Use a clear page structure with landmarks, headings, a search landmark, and list semantics for results. The page should include: ```html Skip to main content
...

Search results

Search legal materials

Search input with autocomplete Active filter chips Sort control

Results

247 results

Loading status region
  • Result card
  • Result card
Pagination controls
``` Semantic decisions: | Area | Semantic Plan | Reason | WCAG Citation | |---|---|---|---| | Page content | `main` with `id="main-content"` | Gives screen reader and keyboard users a clear destination | WCAG 1.3.1, 2.4.1 | | Search interface | `role="search"` wrapping input, filters, and sort | Identifies the search area as a search landmark | WCAG 1.3.1, 4.1.2 | | Results area | `section aria-labelledby="results-heading"` | Groups count, loading state, list, and pagination | WCAG 1.3.1 | | Result cards | `ul` / `li` | Results are a collection, not tabular data | WCAG 1.3.1 | | Sort control | Native `select` with visible `label` | Native form semantics communicate current option | WCAG 1.3.1, 3.3.2, 4.1.2 | | Skip link | First focusable element | Allows bypassing repeated navigation | WCAG 2.4.1 | Do not use a table for result cards. Legal search results have structured fields, but the user is browsing a list of records, not comparing rows and columns. A table would add navigation overhead without providing meaningful tabular relationships. --- ## Interaction Pattern Design ### Interactive Elements Table | Widget | APG Pattern | Keyboard Model | ARIA Attributes | WCAG Citation | |---|---|---|---|---| | Search input | Native text input; autocomplete may follow APG Combobox if suggestions are interactive: https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/patterns/combobox/ | Tab focuses input; typing updates query after debounce; Down Arrow enters suggestions if implemented; Enter submits/selects | If autocomplete suggestions are interactive: `role="combobox"`, `aria-expanded`, `aria-controls`, `aria-activedescendant`, `aria-autocomplete="list"` | WCAG 2.1.1, 1.3.1, 4.1.2 | | Autocomplete suggestions | APG Combobox/Listbox if suggestions can be selected | Arrow keys move through suggestions; Enter selects; Escape closes | `role="listbox"` and `role="option"` if custom suggestions are used | WCAG 2.1.1, 4.1.2 | | Filter chips | APG Button / Toggle Button: https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/patterns/button/ | Tab to each chip; Enter/Space removes or toggles chip | `aria-pressed="true"` while active if chip is a toggle; accessible name includes filter category and value | WCAG 2.1.1, 2.4.6, 4.1.2 | | Sort control | Native select, not ARIA sort | Tab focuses select; Arrow keys change option; Enter/Space opens native picker depending on browser/AT | Visible `label`; no `aria-sort` | WCAG 1.3.1, 2.1.1, 3.3.2, 4.1.2 | | Result count | Status message | Not focusable; announced when text changes | `aria-live="polite"`, `aria-atomic="true"`, `aria-relevant="additions text"` | WCAG 4.1.3 | | Loading state | Status message + busy container | Not focusable; announced without moving focus | `aria-busy="true"` on results section; `role="status"` or polite live text for “Loading results” | WCAG 4.1.3 | | Result links | Native links | Tab reaches each result title link; Enter opens result | Descriptive link text; no generic “Read more” alone | WCAG 2.1.1, 2.4.4 | | Pagination | Native links/buttons; may follow navigation landmark | Tab through page controls; Enter activates | `nav aria-label="Results pagination"`; current page uses `aria-current="page"` | WCAG 1.3.1, 2.4.4, 4.1.2 | ### Sort Control Correction Do not use `aria-sort` on the sort ` ``` WCAG: 1.3.1 Info and Relationships, 3.3.2 Labels or Instructions, 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value. --- ## Live Region Strategy The result count should be the primary live region for search result updates. Plan: ```html

247 results

``` Decisions: | Attribute | Decision | Reason | WCAG Citation | |---|---|---|---| | `aria-live="polite"` | Required | Result counts should not interrupt typing or screen reader speech | WCAG 4.1.3 | | `aria-atomic="true"` | Required | Screen readers should announce the full phrase, e.g. “12 results,” not only the changed number | WCAG 4.1.3 | | `aria-relevant="additions text"` | Use on the count region | The important update is text replacement/addition in the count, not every result card DOM removal/addition | WCAG 4.1.3 | | `role="alert"` | Do not use | Assertive interruption would be noisy during typing and filter changes | WCAG 4.1.3 | | Per-result live regions | Do not use | Re-rendered cards would flood announcements | WCAG 4.1.3 | The 300ms debounce must apply before the count text changes in the DOM. The UI can show visual loading immediately, but live count text should update only after the debounced query resolves. This avoids pushing every keystroke into the NVDA/JAWS live region queue. Recommended sequence: 1. User types. 2. Query state debounces for 300ms. 3. Results section sets `aria-busy="true"`. 4. Polite status says “Loading results” once. 5. Fetch resolves. 6. Results DOM updates. 7. Results section sets `aria-busy="false"`. 8. Result count text updates once, e.g. “12 results.” 9. Screen reader announces the final count politely. If multiple changes happen together, such as removing a filter and changing sort, coalesce announcements into one final count update. Do not announce each intermediate state. --- ## Focus Management Plan Focus should usually remain where the user initiated the change. | Scenario | Focus Behavior | Reason | WCAG Citation | |---|---|---|---| | User types in search input | Focus stays in input | Moving focus on every result update would interrupt typing | WCAG 2.4.3 | | User selects autocomplete suggestion | Focus may stay in input after selection; suggestions close | Maintains search context | WCAG 2.4.3 | | User removes filter chip | Focus moves to the next chip, or previous chip if removed chip was last; if no chips remain, focus moves to search input or filter controls heading | Prevents focus loss after dynamic removal | WCAG 2.4.3 | | User changes sort select | Focus stays on select | User needs confirmation through count/status, not focus movement | WCAG 2.4.3 | | Results reload | Focus does not move to result list automatically | Live count communicates update without hijacking navigation | WCAG 2.4.3, 4.1.3 | | Pagination activated | Focus should move to results heading or result count after new page loads | Page navigation changes result context | WCAG 2.4.3 | The results container must not receive focus merely because the count changed. Only pagination or explicit “jump to results” actions should move focus. For filter chip removal, store the intended next focus target before removal. After React re-renders, focus the next available chip. If no chip remains, use the search input or a stable filter control. This avoids leaving focus on a removed DOM node. Skip link: ```html Skip to main content ``` The skip link must be visible on focus and must move focus to `main`. WCAG 2.4.1. --- ## State Communication Design ### State Communication Table | State | Visual Indicator | Programmatic Indicator | ARIA / HTML Attribute | WCAG Citation | |---|---|---|---|---| | Search idle | Normal input and count visible | Input has label; count text present | `label for`, live count region | WCAG 1.3.1, 3.3.2 | | Search loading | Spinner plus visible “Loading results” text | Results section marked busy; polite status announces loading | `aria-busy="true"`; `role="status"` or `aria-live="polite"` | WCAG 4.1.3 | | Search loaded | Spinner removed; updated count visible | Results section no longer busy; count updates atomically | `aria-busy="false"`; count live region updates | WCAG 4.1.3 | | No results | “No results found” message | Count/status announces “0 results” and no-results text is present in DOM | live count region; list replaced by message | WCAG 1.3.1, 4.1.3 | | Filter active | Chip visible with selected styling | Toggle state exposed | `aria-pressed="true"` on active toggle chip | WCAG 4.1.2 | | Filter removed | Chip disappears; count changes | Focus moves to next logical control; final count announced | focus management plus live count | WCAG 2.4.3, 4.1.3 | | Sort selected | Selected option visible | Native select exposes selected option | `label` + selected `
